title: Soy jar candle — lavender and pine
subtitle: A fresh forest-floor blend with a calming edge
excerpt: A soy-wax container candle scented with lavender and Scots pine essential oils. The combination reads as the smell of a pine wood after rain: green and resinous with a soft lavender underpinning.
sourceNotes: Scots pine (Pinus sylvestris) essential oil flash point: above 65°C. Lavender essential oil flash point: approximately 70 to 75°C. Both add safely at soy container pour temperature (60 to 65°C). Fragrance load 7% of wax weight within the 6 to 8% standard soy container range.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 jar candle (~200 ml, approximately 30 hours burn time)
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Allow at least 24 hours before first lighting. One week of cure is recommended.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Store away from sunlight and heat. Lavender top note fades within 18 to 24 months; pine is a sturdier note and holds longer. The wax keeps indefinitely.

glossaryTerms.definition: The temperature at which a fragrance or essential oil can ignite near a flame. Pine and lavender have flash points above the standard soy container pour temperature.
The first burn of a jar candle, which should last until the melt pool reaches the jar edge, preventing tunnelling on subsequent burns.
The rest period after pouring during which fragrance oil binds to the wax. Soy container candles benefit from 1 to 2 weeks of cure before first use.

content.text: Scots pine essential oil has a fresh, resinous, slightly camphorous quality that is distinct from the sharper, more medicinal smell of eucalyptus. Paired with lavender, the pine note gives the combination a green, forested quality while the lavender softens it. Both essential oils have

values above the soy container pour temperature of 60 to 65°C, so there is no flash-point concern at the add-in stage. The total fragrance load is 7% of wax weight: 4.5% lavender and 2.5% pine. A week of

items.prepNote: 4.5% of wax weight; main floral note
2.5% of wax weight; green, resinous depth
Sized for a 200 ml jar; CD-12 as a starting point

content.text: Warm the jar gently. Secure the wick to the base and thread through the centring bar.

Melt the soy wax in the pouring pitcher over low heat. Do not leave unattended. Remove from heat once fully melted. Let the wax cool to 60 to 65°C. Add lavender and pine essential oils and stir for 30 seconds. Pour into the jar, leaving a 1 cm gap at the top. Leave undisturbed for at least 4 hours. Top up any sink hole around the wick with a small second pour at the same temperature.
Trim the wick to 5 mm. Burn until the
is complete, with the melt pool reaching the jar edge. This takes approximately 3 to 4 hours for a 200 ml jar. Subsequent burns should follow the same pattern.
Store away from sunlight and heat. The lavender note fades before the pine over time; after 2 years the candle may smell primarily of pine. Keep the lid on when not in use.
